Crude_Oil.description: Complex liquid hydrocarbon feedstock refined into fuels, petrochemical feedstocks, base oils, waxes, coke, sulfur, and asphalt.

children.Methanol: reforming to syngas followed by methanol synthesis
children.Ammonia: reforming to hydrogen followed by Haber-Bosch synthesis
children.Urea: ammonia synthesis followed by urea synthesis

Natural_Gas.description: Gaseous hydrocarbon feedstock dominated by methane with recoverable ethane, propane, butanes, and condensate.
Natural_Gas.typical_uses: fuel gas, hydrogen production, ammonia, methanol, olefins feedstock

Natural_Gas_Liquids.description: Recovered ethane, propane, butanes, and natural gasoline used as petrochemical and refinery feedstocks.

children.Blue_Hydrogen: steam methane reforming with carbon capture

Naphtha.description: Light refinery liquid used as a steam cracker feedstock, reformer feedstock, and gasoline blending component.
Naphtha.typical_uses: ethylene production, aromatics production, gasoline blending

Sulfur.description: Recovered by-product from hydrodesulfurization and acid gas processing.

children.Cumene_Hydroperoxide: cumene route via alkylation and oxidation

Benzene.description: BTX aromatic used for styrene, cumene, cyclohexane, aniline, alkylbenzene, and maleic anhydride.
Benzene.typical_uses: polystyrene, phenol, nylon intermediates, polyurethane intermediates, detergents

Toluene.description: Aromatic solvent and feedstock for TDI, benzene, xylenes, and specialty derivatives.
Toluene.typical_uses: solvents, TDI, benzene conversion, gasoline blending

children.Polyester_Fiber: PX oxidation to PTA followed by polyester polycondensation

Para_Xylene.description: Xylene isomer oxidized to purified terephthalic acid for polyester and PET.
Para_Xylene.typical_uses: PTA, PET, polyester fiber, polyester film

parents.Ethylene: alkylation with benzene followed by dehydrogenation via ethylbenzene

children.ABS: graft copolymerization with acrylonitrile and butadiene

children.Nylon_6: oxidation to cyclohexanone followed by caprolactam route
children.Nylon_66: oxidation to adipic acid followed by polycondensation

children.Polyester_Fiber: polycondensation with ethylene glycol and spinning

children.Alkyd_Resin: esterification with polyols and fatty acids

Phthalic_Anhydride.description: Aromatic anhydride used for phthalate plasticizers, alkyd resins, and unsaturated polyester resins.

children.Styrene: alkylation with benzene followed by dehydrogenation via ethylbenzene

Ethylene.description: Primary light olefin and major building block for polyethylene, ethylene oxide, ethylene dichloride, vinyl acetate, and styrene.
Ethylene.typical_uses: polyethylene, PVC chain, PET chain, solvents, surfactants

children.Propylene_Impact_Copolymer: impact copolymerization with ethylene rubber phase
children.Polyolefin_Elastomer: copolymerization with ethylene and alpha olefins
children.Butanol: oxo synthesis via hydroformylation and hydrogenation

Propylene.description: Primary light olefin used for polypropylene, propylene oxide, acrylonitrile, acrylic acid, oxo alcohols, and cumene.

children.ABS: graft copolymerization with acrylonitrile and styrene

Butadiene.description: C4 diolefin recovered from steam cracking and used mainly in synthetic rubbers and ABS.
Butadiene.typical_uses: SBR, polybutadiene rubber, nitrile rubber, ABS

children.Propylene_Glycol_Monomethyl_Ether_Acetat: esterification with propylene glycol monomethyl ether

Ethylene_Oxide.description: Reactive epoxide from ethylene oxidation, used mainly for glycols and ethoxylates.
Ethylene_Oxide.typical_uses: ethylene glycol, surfactants, ethanolamines, glycol ethers

children.Polyester_Fiber: polycondensation with PTA followed by spinning

Polyvinyl_Chloride.description: Chlorinated vinyl polymer made from vinyl chloride monomer.
Polyvinyl_Chloride.typical_uses: pipes, profiles, flooring, cable insulation, films

Polyethylene.description: Major polyolefin polymer produced from ethylene in LDPE, LLDPE, and HDPE grades.
Polyethylene.typical_uses: film, packaging, pipes, containers, wire and cable

Polypropylene.description: Major polyolefin polymer produced from propylene in homopolymer, random copolymer, and impact copolymer grades.
Polypropylene.typical_uses: packaging, fibers, automotive parts, appliances, medical products

Propylene_Oxide.description: Epoxide from propylene used for polyether polyols, propylene glycol, and glycol ethers.

children.ABS: graft copolymerization with butadiene and styrene

Acrylonitrile.description: Nitrile monomer produced by propylene ammoxidation and used in acrylic fiber, ABS, nitrile rubber, and adiponitrile.
Acrylonitrile.typical_uses: ABS, acrylic fiber, nitrile rubber, carbon fiber precursor

parents.Propylene: oxo synthesis via hydroformylation and hydrogenation

parents.Benzene: cumene route via alkylation and oxidation

children.Vinyl_Ester_Resin: reaction with acrylic or methacrylic acid

parents.Styrene: graft copolymerization with acrylonitrile and butadiene
parents.Acrylonitrile: graft copolymerization with butadiene and styrene
parents.Butadiene: graft copolymerization with acrylonitrile and styrene

children.Tire_Rubber_Compound: compounding with carbon black and oil

Styrene_Butadiene_Rubber.description: Synthetic rubber produced by copolymerizing styrene and butadiene.

children.Tire_Rubber_Compound: compounding with carbon black and oil

parents.Natural_Gas: reforming to syngas followed by methanol synthesis

children.Methyl_Methacrylate: ACH, C4 oxidation, or ethylene route

Methanol.description: C1 alcohol made from syngas and used for formaldehyde, acetic acid, MTBE, DME, methylamines, and olefins.
Methanol.typical_uses: formaldehyde, acetic acid, MTBE, MTO, solvents, fuels

children.Ammonia: hydrogen production followed by Haber-Bosch synthesis

Syngas.description: Mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide used for methanol, ammonia hydrogen, oxo chemicals, and Fischer-Tropsch liquids.
Syngas.typical_uses: methanol, ammonia, oxo alcohols, synthetic fuels

parents.Syngas: hydrogen production followed by Haber-Bosch synthesis
parents.Natural_Gas: reforming to hydrogen followed by Haber-Bosch synthesis

Ammonia.description: Nitrogen chemical made from hydrogen and nitrogen, mainly used for fertilizers and nitrates.
Ammonia.typical_uses: urea, ammonium nitrate, nitric acid, MAP, DAP

parents.Natural_Gas: ammonia synthesis followed by urea synthesis

Urea.description: Nitrogen fertilizer produced from ammonia and carbon dioxide.

children.MDI: condensation with aniline followed by phosgenation

parents.Methanol: ACH, C4 oxidation, or ethylene route

PET.description: Polyester polymer made from PTA or DMT and ethylene glycol.

parents.Purified_Terephthalic_Acid: polycondensation with ethylene glycol and spinning
parents.Ethylene_Glycol: polycondensation with PTA followed by spinning
parents.Para_Xylene: PX oxidation to PTA followed by polyester polycondensation

Linear_Alkylbenzene.description: Detergent intermediate produced by alkylating benzene with linear olefins or chloroparaffins.

parents.Methane: steam methane reforming with carbon capture
